chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ بَدَأَ بِالْمَرْوَةِ قَبْلَ الصَّفَا أَوْ سَهَا فِي السَّعْيِ بَيْنَهُمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 143 – The one who begins with Al-Marwa before Al-Safa, or makes a mistake in the Sa’ee between the two

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/76)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/76)
text: مَنْ طَافَ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَ الْمَرْوَةِ خَمْسَةَ عَشَرَ شَوْطاً طَرَحَ ثَمَانِيَةً وَ اعْتَدَّ بِسَبْعَةٍ وَ إِنْ بَدَأَ بِالْمَرْوَةِ فَلْيَطْرَحْ وَ لْيَبْدَأْ بِالصَّفَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, and Safwan Bin Yahya, from Muawiya Bin Ammar who said,

‘The one who performs Tawaaf between Al-Safa and Al-Marwa by fifteen circuits, eight would be dropped and he would be counted with seven; and if he began with Al-Marwa, so let him drop (it all) and let him begin with Al-Safa’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Safwan ibn Yahya from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one performs fifteen times instead of seven times walking between al-Safa’ and al-Marwah he drops eight Shawt (walking once between al-Safa’ and al- Marwah) and counts the seven. If he begins from al-Marwah he must drop it and begin from al- Safa’.’”
